faqs.a: DMDD, or disruptive mood dysregulation disorder, is a childhood diagnosis added to the DSM in 2013. It involves severe, frequent temper outbursts plus a persistently irritable or angry mood between them, lasting at least a year and showing up in more than one setting.

faqs.q: How is DMDD different from bipolar disorder?
faqs.a: Bipolar disorder involves distinct episodes of mania or hypomania. DMDD involves chronic, ongoing irritability without those episodes. The diagnosis was added partly so children with chronic irritability weren't labeled bipolar by default.

faqs.q: At what age is DMDD diagnosed?
faqs.a: DMDD can be diagnosed between ages 6 and 18, and symptoms have to start before age 10. It's meant specifically for children rather than adults.
